headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 6.1 million barrels during the week ending June 19, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. headlines.title: UK Climate Panel Urges Faster Electrification to Lower Energy Bills
headlines.summary: UK’s slower-than-expected electrification exposes households to fossil fuel price shocks, the country’s Climate Change Committee (CCC) said on Wednesday, urging faster electrification and removal of policy costs to lower the energy bills. headlines.title: Euro Sinks To One-Year Low As Oil Price Drop Fuels ECB Rate Cut Bets
headlines.summary: The euro has sunk to a one-year low, with easing of tensions between the United States and Iran helping to cool oil prices and raise expectations that the European Central Bank will turn more dovish. The euro was quoted at $1.135 against the U.S. dollar on Wednesday, down from $1

headlines.title: India Cuts Coal Imports as Power Plants Turn to Domestic Fuel
headlines.summary: India is boosting the share of domestic coal used in power plants designated to run on imported fuel, as rising local coal production and expanding renewables have helped to cut coal imports to multi-year lows. India has raised the use of domestic coal to 50% at many power plants

headlines.title: Qatar Says LNG Exports Could Return to Normal Within Weeks
headlines.summary: Qatar will be back to normal production of liquefied natural gas in a few weeks, the country’s prime minister, Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani, told the Financial Times today. “Within a few weeks, production will come back to normal, except the damaged facility,” he said

headlines.title: Why the Next Billion Barrels of Oil Demand Could Come From Storage
headlines.summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and the stranding of more than 10 million barrels per day (bpd) of crude oil in the Persian Gulf was a wake-up call for import-dependent countries to expand their capacity to hold strategic and commercial reserves. headlines.title: The AI Boom Is Set to Fast-Track China's Coming Nuclear Energy Dominance
